headline: Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices, Iran War Drive Stocks Lower; Nvidia GTC, Micron Earnings Due
summary: The stock market fell for a third straight week, breaking or testing key levels, as oil prices soared amid the Iran war. Nvidia GTC and Micron earnings loom.

headline: SanDisk Climbs 6% as Sector-Wide Memory Shortage Fuels Fresh Investor Optimism
summary: SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) is climbing roughly 6% in Friday trading, with shares touching $655 as of midday. The move extends a powerful run that has made SNDK one of the most talked-about names in the market this week. The catalyst is familiar: a structural shortage in NAND flash memory, supercharged by AI infrastructure spending, continues to ... headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Adobe, Tesla, Ulta Beauty, SentinelOne, and More
summary: Stock futures were moving slightly to the upside Friday as oil prices rose and Wall Street awaited the release of personal consumption expenditures price index data for February.  Tesla  rose 0.6% in premarket trading.  Better-than-expected fiscal first-quarter earnings were overshadowed by the resignation of the software company’s long-time CEO, Shantanu Narayen.

headline: 2 Reasons to Like WDC and 1 to Stay Skeptical
summary: What a fantastic six months it’s been for Western Digital. Shares of the company have skyrocketed 155%, hitting $260.97. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.

headline: HP Enterprise confirms memory shortage will last longer than expected
summary: “I keep thinking this has got to end, but we got confirmation this week from HP Enterprise that it’s going to go on for much longer than people think.” That’s Jim Cramer, and the “it” he’s referring to is the global memory shortage that has quietly become one of the most consequential supply stories in ... headline: Has Western Digital (WDC) Run Too Far After Its Multi‑Year Share Price Surge?
summary: If you are wondering whether Western Digital's share price still offers value after a big run, this article will walk through what the numbers actually say about the stock. Western Digital recently closed at US$261.18, with returns of 0.8% over 7 days, a 0.5% decline over 30 days, 39.1% year to date and a very large gain over 1 year and 3 years, plus a 401.9% return over 5 years. Recent coverage has focused on Western Digital as investors reassess storage and memory related names, tying...
